refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable the operator (on-site or remote) to execute earthmoving tasks with measurable productivity of ≥ productivity_target (m³/h or ton/h) during representative operations over the agreed evaluation period, while maintaining compliance with worksite safety plan and operational policies.
refined_fr.rationale: CR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that conflates multiple concerns: task execution capability, operator interface (on-site vs. remote), measurement/telemetry, and safety compliance. Refinement separates the productivity outcome from the enabling mechanisms (teleoperation, safety services, vehicle control) and anchors verification to measurable, time-bound evaluation criteria.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Conduct representative operations trial over agreed period (e.g., 8-hour shift, 5-day week). Log task completion (volume moved, tonnage hauled), elapsed time, and safety incidents. Compare achieved productivity against productivity_target. Verify compliance with worksite safety plan via incident log and safety manager sign-off.
sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (volume/time, tonnage/time) to the Site Manager.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below target.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Integrate high-payload vehicle with low-latency teleoperation and real-time productivity telemetry; enforce safety constraints via software governors.
DP-B: Deploy vehicle with on-site operator only; add productivity logging via onboard sensors; rely on operator training and worksite safety plan for safety.
DP-C: Hybrid: on-site operator for routine tasks, teleoperation for hazardous/remote tasks; unified telemetry and safety services across both modes.
DP-D: Maximize vehicle engine power and bucket capacity; minimize cycle time via aggressive hydraulic tuning; accept higher safety risk and rely on operator skill.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C: Hybrid operator model (on-site + teleoperation) with unified telemetry and safety services. On-site operator handles routine earthmoving; teleoperation enables remote operation for hazardous/confined areas. Both modes feed real-time productivity metrics and safety alerts to a centralized Safety Services module. Safety constraints (speed governors, geofence, emergency stop) are enforced uniformly across both modes via the Safety Services module.

potential_sources: Teleoperation latency may couple with vehicle responsiveness; mitigated by low-latency network link and predictive control algorithms.
Safety Services module must couple with both Vehicle and Teleoperation to enforce constraints; this is intentional and managed via SafetyAlert interface.
Productivity telemetry depends on accurate sensor data from Vehicle; coupling is acceptable and necessary.
coupling_check.notes: DP-C avoids over-coupling by separating concerns: Vehicle owns task execution; Teleoperation owns remote control interface; Safety Services owns constraint enforcement; NGHE Solution owns orchestration and productivity reporting. Interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) decouple these modules.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Network link latency is ≤ 200 ms round-trip for teleoperation to be safe and responsive. Risk: If latency exceeds threshold, teleoperation mode may be unsafe or unproductive.
Assumption: On-site operator and tele-operator are trained and certified. Risk: Operator error in either mode could violate safety plan or degrade productivity.
Assumption: Safety Services module can enforce constraints without introducing unacceptable latency or mechanical failure modes. Risk: Constraint enforcement (e.g., emergency stop) must be fail-safe.

Assumption: Productivity target is achievable with hybrid model without excessive mode-switching overhead. Risk: Frequent switching between on-site and teleoperation could reduce overall productivity.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-C to specify: (1) Network link SLA (latency, availability, bandwidth); (2) Operator mode-switching protocol and training requirements; (3) Safety Services constraint enforcement logic (e.g., speed governor algorithm, geofence boundary definition); (4) Telemetry data schema and sampling rate for productivity calculation.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Top-level system integration module that orchestrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services to deliver hybrid operator model with unified telemetry and safety enforcement. Owns productivity KPI calculation, operator mode management, and system-level safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution emits productivity metrics (volume/time, tonnage/time, cycle time, safety events) to Site Manager dashboard and Maintenance/Quality systems.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Site Manager or Tele-operator sends task commands (dig, load, haul, dump, mode-switch) to NGHE Solution, which routes to Vehicle or Teleoperation module.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution (via Safety Services) emits safety alerts (geofence breach, speed limit exceeded, emergency stop triggered) to Safety Manager and operator.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution maintains network connectivity for teleoperation, telemetry uplink, and safety alert distribution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level container for the entire productivity KPI requirement. It owns orchestration, telemetry aggregation, and system-level safety compliance. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) cross the boundary.

Vehicle owns task execution (dig, load, haul, dump) and onboard sensors. However, productivity KPI calculation, operator mode management, and safety constraint enforcement are system-level concerns that belong in NGHE Solution, not Vehicle. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Vehicle alone would diffuse safety and telemetry responsibilities.

Teleoperation owns remote operator interface and control latency. However, it does not own on-site operator mode, productivity metrics, or safety enforcement. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Teleoperation alone would exclude on-site operations and safety services.

Safety Services owns constraint enforcement (speed governors, geofence, emergency stop) and safety alert generation. However, it does not own task execution, operator interface, or productivity metrics. Assigning FR-NGHE-001 to Safety Services alone would exclude vehicle control and telemetry.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary because: (1) it owns the top-level orchestration of hybrid operator model (on-site + teleoperation); (2) it aggregates telemetry from Vehicle and Safety Services to calculate productivity KPI; (3) it manages operator mode-switching and task routing; (4) it enforces system-level safety compliance via Safety Services. Sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) are contained within NGHE Solution and communicate via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link).
containment_risks: Risk: NGHE Solution may become a monolithic orchestration layer if not carefully decomposed. Mitigation: Define clear ownership boundaries for Vehicle (task execution), Teleoperation (remote control), and Safety Services (constraint enforcement). Use interface contracts to decouple.

Risk: Productivity KPI calculation may depend on data from multiple sources (Vehicle sensors, Teleoperation logs, Safety Services events). If data schema is not standardized, integration will be fragile. Mitigation: Define Telemetry interface schema upfront.

Risk: Operator mode-switching (on-site to teleoperation) may introduce race conditions or safety gaps if not carefully sequenced. Mitigation: Define mode-switching protocol and state machine in NGHE Solution design.

Risk: Network Link failure may prevent teleoperation and telemetry uplink, degrading productivity and safety visibility. Mitigation: Define fallback behavior (e.g., on-site operator takes control, local safety enforcement continues).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to specify: (1) Internal module structure (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, Orchestration layer); (2) Interface contracts (Telemetry schema, Tasking protocol, SafetyAlert format, Network Link SLA); (3) Operator mode-switching state machine and safety interlocks; (4) Productivity KPI calculation algorithm and data sources; (5) Fallback behavior for network failure or safety service degradation.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (hybrid operator model with unified telemetry and safety services) directly addresses FR-NGHE-001 (productivity KPI). DP-C enables both on-site and remote operation, supports real-time productivity measurement, and enforces safety constraints uniformly. No coupling issues detected. Assumptions and risks are documented and manageable.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry diffusion: Productivity metrics must be aggregated from Vehicle sensors, Teleoperation logs, and Safety Services events. Mitigation: Define Telemetry interface schema and data ownership upfront.

Safety constraint enforcement diffusion: Speed governors, geofence, and emergency stop may be implemented in Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Mitigation: Centralize constraint logic in Safety Services; Vehicle and Teleoperation call Safety Services APIs.

Operator mode-switching diffusion: Mode-switching logic may be scattered across NGHE Solution, Teleoperation, and Vehicle. Mitigation: Centralize mode-switching state machine in NGHE Solution orchestration layer.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct IM boundary with containment score 95. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are sub-modules with clear ownership. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and decouple the system from external stakeholders (Site Manager, Safety Manager, Maintenance/Quality).

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: FR-NGHE-001 is verifiable via representative operations trial: measure productivity (m³/h or ton/h) over agreed period, compare against productivity_target, and verify safety compliance via incident log. NGHE Solution IM boundary owns telemetry aggregation and KPI calculation, making verification feasible.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: FR-NGHE-001 is allocated to NGHE Solution IM boundary. Sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4) are allocated to Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution respectively. Allocation is clear and traceable.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-C to specify network link SLA (latency ≤ 200 ms, availability ≥ 99.5%, bandwidth ≥ 10 Mbps) to ensure teleoperation safety and responsiveness.
Refine NGHE Solution IM boundary to specify internal module structure, interface contracts, and operator mode-switching state machine.
Define Telemetry interface schema (data fields, sampling rate, timestamp format) to enable reliable productivity KPI calculation.
Define fallback behavior for network failure, Safety Services degradation, and operator mode-switching errors.

decision.justification: FR-NGHE-001 is a high-level KPI requirement that has been successfully refined into atomic sub-FRs (FR-NGHE-001.1 through FR-NGHE-001.4), each addressing a specific aspect of the productivity target: vehicle task execution, operator interface, telemetry, and safety constraints. DP-C (hybrid operator model) is a sound design choice that couples well with NGHE Solution IM boundary. However, the requirement is not atomic and requires further decomposition to allocate sub-FRs to sub-modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) and define detailed design parameters (network SLA, telemetry schema, mode-switching protocol). Proceed to next decomposition level for each sub-FR.
decision.justification_detail: The refined FR is complex and multi-faceted. While DP-C and NGHE Solution IM are well-justified, the sub-FRs require detailed design work at the next level. Specifically: (1) FR-NGHE-001.1 (vehicle task execution) must be decomposed into hydraulic system, engine, transmission, and bucket control DPs; (2) FR-NGHE-001.2 (operator interface) must be decomposed into on-site cabin and teleoperation control station DPs; (3) FR-NGHE-001.3 (telemetry) must be decomposed into sensor, data aggregation, and KPI calculation DPs; (4) FR-NGHE-001.4 (safety constraints) must be decomposed into speed governor, geofence, and emergency stop DPs. Each sub-FR and DP pair must be evaluated for coupling, assumptions, and IM assignment.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-001.1: The vehicle shall execute earthmoving tasks (dig, load, haul, dump) with repeatable cycle time and payload capacity.
FR-NGHE-001.2: The operator interface (on-site or teleoperation) shall provide sufficient situational awareness and control responsiveness to sustain target cycle time.
FR-NGHE-001.3: The NGHE solution shall continuously monitor and report productivity metrics (volume/time, tonnage/time) to the Site Manager.
FR-NGHE-001.4: The NGHE solution shall enforce safety constraints (speed limits, geofence, emergency stop) that do not degrade productivity below target.

assumptions: Network link latency is ≤ 200 ms round-trip for teleoperation to be safe and responsive.
On-site operator and tele-operator are trained and certified per worksite safety plan.
Safety Services module can enforce constraints without introducing unacceptable latency or mechanical failure modes.
Productivity target is achievable with hybrid model without excessive mode-switching overhead.
Telemetry data from Vehicle sensors is accurate and available in real-time.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies are well-defined and communicated to all stakeholders.
open_risks: Network link failure may prevent teleoperation and telemetry uplink, degrading productivity and safety visibility.
Operator mode-switching (on-site to teleoperation) may introduce race conditions or safety gaps if not carefully sequenced.
Productivity KPI calculation may depend on data from multiple sources; if data schema is not standardized, integration will be fragile.
NGHE Solution may become a monolithic orchestration layer if not carefully decomposed.
Teleoperation latency may couple with vehicle responsiveness, affecting cycle time and productivity.
Safety constraint enforcement may be diffused across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services, creating maintenance and verification challenges.
evidence_needed: Network link SLA specification (latency, availability, bandwidth) validated against teleoperation safety and responsiveness requirements.
Operator mode-switching protocol and state machine design, validated via simulation or prototype testing.
Telemetry interface schema (data fields, sampling rate, timestamp format) and data ownership matrix.
Safety Services constraint enforcement logic (speed governor algorithm, geofence boundary definition, emergency stop sequence) validated via FMEA or safety analysis.
Representative operations trial data: productivity metrics (m³/h or ton/h), cycle time, safety incidents, and operator feedback.
Worksite safety plan and operational policies documentation, signed by Safety Manager and Site Manager.
